message:
feat: injectable time source for GenericPool lifecycle + router load comparison optimization

Pool (src/sync/pool.rs):
- Add `GenericPool::with_time_getter(factory, config, time_getter)` constructor
  and `time_getter()` accessor so all lifecycle bookkeeping (idle eviction,
  hold duration, warmup timestamps, acquire metrics) uses the injected clock
  instead of Instant::now().
- Add `PooledResource::new_with_time_getter()` constructor that threads the
  time source through to held_duration().
- Update `new_with_created_at()` to accept a time_getter parameter.
- All internal Instant::now() calls replaced with (self.time_getter)().
- Thread-local test clock infrastructure: `test_pool_time_now()`,
  `reset_test_pool_time()`, `set_test_pool_time_offset()`,
  `advance_test_pool_time()` using Cell-based storage to avoid cross-test
  interference.
- Migrate `idle_eviction_respects_idle_timeout` and
  `idle_eviction_respects_max_lifetime` tests from thread::sleep to
  deterministic clock advancement.
- New test: `warmup_created_timestamps_follow_time_getter` verifies that
  warmup-created resources use the injected clock for their created_at
  timestamps so they don't appear immediately expired.
- Update `generic_pool_held_duration_increases` to use exact deterministic
  assertion instead of >= comparison.

Router (src/transport/router.rs):
- Downcast u128 → u64 in compare_weighted_load() since connection counts
  and weights are u32, and u64 multiplication is sufficient and faster on
  most platforms.
- Same u128 → u64 downcast in WeightedLeastConnections select path.
- Add early break in LeastConnections when a zero-connection endpoint is
  found, avoiding unnecessary iteration through remaining endpoints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
